titleStmt.title: SII 9.26: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 26. (A.R. No. 726 of 1916.) ON A SLAB SET UP IN THE TOPE TO THE NORTH OF THE SAME TEMPLE.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 872 (= A.D. 950-51) without any other details and refers itself to the reign of the Noḻamba-Pallava king Iṟiva-Noḻamba. It states that when Mallaṇa Sivari was in charge of Chirapi twelve, Gāvunḍa Kāḍiyaṇṇa and Paḷḷikāṟa Tuvaṇṇa were each granted 3 kaṇḍugas of wet land for repairing the Oḷageṟe tank. The gift was made in the presence of the inhabitants of the four villages Chirupi, Chintakuṇṭe, Chiṟiyavoḻalu and Vuḷuvaṭṭu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . sakanripakālātītasaṁvatsa
rasataṁgaḷeṇṭanūṟa Eḻpatteraḍu
svasti samadhigatapañcamahāśa
bda pallavānvaya śrīpritivīvalla
bha pallavarāma palaroḷegaṇḍa
nēkavākhyaṁ śrīmatu Īṟivanoḻambha
pritivīrājyaṅgeyuttire cirapipa
nneraḍu mallaṇasivariyāḷe
Oḷageṟeya kaṭṭi paḍeda sāsana
cirupiyu cintakuṇṭeyu ci
ṟiyavoḻalu vuḷuvaṭṭu
nālkūrumiḻdu naḍeda kelli
goḍaṁgeyoḍḍina keḻage gā
vuṇḍakāḍiyaṇnaṅge mūgaṇḍu
gaṅgaḻde paḷḷikāṟa tuvaṇnaṅge
mūgaṇḍugaṅgaḻde Ida salalī
yadavaru vāranāsiyuṁ kavi
leyumanaḻido . Lines 17 and 18 are written
Digital edition of SII 9.26 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
